Ukraine Conflict News





06 September 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • CENTCOM Provides Life-Saving Assistance Following Flooding in Pakistan CENTCOM 06 Sep 2025 -- U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) provided urgent, life-saving assistance to Pakistan on September 5, 2025, to deliver essential relief to civilians impacted by the recent catastrophic flooding.
  • In response to the ongoing flood situation, the United States of America through Army Central Command (US ARCENT) has extended humanitarian assistance for the flood-affected population of Pakistan. ISPR Pakistan 06 Sep 2025

Defense Policy / Programs

  • US Navy SEALs killed North Korean civilians in failed Trump-approved mission: Report Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- US Navy SEALs killed several North Korean civilians during a failed 2019 mission to plant a listening device, an operation approved by then-President Donald Trump amid high-stakes diplomatic negotiations between Washington and Pyongyang in 2019, a US news outlet reports.
  • Trump Says Knows Nothing About Alleged Failed Top-Secret Operation in North Korea in 2019 Sputnik 06 Sep 2025 -- US President Donald Trump said on Friday that he had heard nothing about an allegedly failed US top-secret operation in North Korea in early 2019.

  • Trump signs executive order reviving "Department of War" though some call it "childish" People's Daily 06 Sep 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Friday to rebrand the Department of Defense as the Department of War, reviving a name that was last used in the late 1940s.

  • Europe Faces $1 Trillion Bill to Replace US Military Power Sputnik 06 Sep 2025 -- The replacement of US military capabilities in the EU will cost Europe one trillion dollars, Bloomberg reports quoting the International Institute for Strategic Studies (IISS).

  • European Leaders To Visit US This Week For Talks On Ukraine War, Trump Says RFE/RL 06 Sep 2025 -- European leaders will visit the United States this week to discuss how to end the war in Ukraine, US President Donald Trump said on September 7 after Russia pounded the Ukrainian capital with a new major drone strike.
  • Slovakia Will Not Send Troops to Ukraine Within Security Guarantees - Prime Minister Sputnik 06 Sep 2025 -- Slovakia will not send troops to Ukraine as part of security guarantees, but is ready to provide logistical assistance, Prime Minister Robert Fico said on Saturday.
  • Among the Top Priorities in All Our Work With Partners Is Stronger Air Defense, Protection Against Russian "Shaheds," Against Russian Missiles - Address by the President President of Ukraine 06 Sep 2025
  • Position of Ministry of Defence on draft laws that sparked public concern Ukraine MOD 06 Sep 2025
  • Putin Warns Against Foreign Troops In Ukraine As Europe Tries To Finalize 'Reassurance Force' RFE/RL 06 Sep 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin has warned against the deployment of any foreign troops into Ukraine, declaring they would be "legitimate targets," in a blunt warning to Kyiv's Western allies as they look to agree on a "reassurance force."
  • A Toxic Trail Exposed: How Russia Makes Chemical Grenades And Uses Them Against Ukraine RFE/RL 06 Sep 2025 -- The website of Russia's Scientific Research Institute for Applied Chemistry (NIIPH) features images of fireworks displays and one of the items it produces: sparklers in packages adorned with drawings of a holiday tree, a Santa Claus figure, and cute kids and critters.

  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding Media Reports on "Armament Bureau's Drone Tender Specifications Not Meeting Operational Requirements" ROC MND 06 Sep 2025

  • INS KADMATT LEADS MOBILE FLEET REVIEW AT PAPUA NEW GUINEA'S 50TH INDEPENDENCE DAY India PIB 06 Sep 2025 -- In a strong display of friendship and maritime partnership, INS Kadmatt, an indigenously designed and built Anti-Submarine Warfare Corvette, was accorded the honour of conducting and leading the mobile Fleet Review on 04 Sep 2025, as part of Papua New Guinea's 50th Independence Day celebrations

  • Iranian, Indian security officials hold talks to boost defense ties IRNA 06 Sep 2025 -- Ali Larijani, Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, held talks with Ajit Doval, India's National Security Advisor, to exchange views on strengthening defense and security relations.

  • Second round of Putin-Trump talks possible in near future: Kremlin People's Daily 06 Sep 2025 -- The second round of talks between Russian President Vladimir Putin and U.S. President Donald Trump may take place in the near future, Kremlin Spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Friday.

  • Trump says not to attend G20 summit in South Africa People's Daily 06 Sep 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Friday that he would not attend the G20 summit in South Africa later this year, but planned to send Vice President JD Vance in his place.

  • Deputy Prime Minister Radosław Sikorski visits Washington Poland - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Sep 2025 -- Poland's efforts to join the G20, presence of US troops in Poland, and security guarantees for Ukraine were the main topics discussed by the head of Polish diplomacy with the representatives of US authorities during meetings held in the US capital on 4-6 September.

  • Trump Signs Order To Label Countries Wrongfully Holding US Citizens As Terror Sponsors RFE/RL 06 Sep 2025 -- US President Donald Trump signed an executive order that could designate countries that have wrongfully detained US citizens as state sponsors of terrorism, including Iran, Afghanistan, Russia, and China.

  • U.S. judge blocks Trump administration's ending of protections for over 1 million Venezuelans, Haitians People's Daily 06 Sep 2025 -- A U.S. federal judge on Friday blocked the Trump administration from ending temporary legal protections for more than 1 million people from Haiti and Venezuela, allowing them to remain in the United States with work authorization.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • IDF Controls Over 80% of Gaza Strip, 90% of Infrastructure Destroyed Sputnik 06 Sep 2025 -- The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) controls more than 80% of the territory of the Gaza Strip, the Palestinian enclave's administration said on Saturday.
  • Six Palestinians, including people waiting for aid, killed by Israeli forces in Gaza Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Six Palestinians, including people waiting for aid, have been killed by the Israeli forces' gunfire and ongoing bombardment in areas across the Gaza Strip.

  • Resistance groups rage against Israeli minister's 'gates of hell' remarks Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Several Palestinian resistance groups have voiced outrage over the Israeli minister for military affairs' threat of Tel Aviv's opening the "gates of hell" on the Gaza Strip.

  • UN rights chief condemns sanctions on Palestinian NGOs helping ICC probe into Israel Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- United Nations human rights chief Volker Turk has rejected the latest United States sanctions on three prominent Palestinian rights groups for their role in supporting the International Criminal Court's (ICC) efforts to prosecute Israeli leaders as "completely unacceptable," calling for them to be withdrawn.
  • UN General Assembly decides to resume high-level int'l conference on two-state solution People's Daily 06 Sep 2025 -- The UN General Assembly (UNGA) on Friday decided to resume a high-level international conference on the two-state solution on Sept. 22, reviving a process that was put on hold this summer amid escalating violence in the Middle East.

  • Pakistan condemns the Israeli occupying power's statements regarding the forced displacement of Palestinians Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Sep 2025 -- Pakistan strongly condemns recent statements by the Israeli occupying power, expressing its intention to forcibly displace Palestinians from their land. Such actions are a clear violation of international law and a deliberate attempt to sabotage efforts for achieving peace and stability in the region.
  • Israel's portrayal of Palestinians' displacement as voluntary 'nonsense': Egypt Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty has reaffirmed his nation's stance against the forced removal of Palestinians from the Gaza Strip, asserting that the Israeli regime's portrayal of the displacement of the besieged enclave's inhabitants as voluntary is "nonsense."
  • Saudi Arabia urges UN Security Council to halt Israel's 'aggressive policies' in Gaza amid displacement plot Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Saudi Arabia has condemned Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's plans to forcibly displace Palestinians from Gaza, urging the UN Security Council to intervene to halt Israel's "aggressive policies" against the Palestinian people and their land.

  • Explainer: Why is a Gaza ceasefire deal so hard to reach? People's Daily 06 Sep 2025 -- Two weeks after Hamas accepted a peace proposal mediated by Egypt and Qatar, Israel has yet to issue a formal response. Instead, it has focused on bolstering its forces around Gaza City in preparation for an expanded offensive to seize the enclave's largest urban center.

    • Other Conflicts

News Reports

  • Venezuela's Maduro warns of 'armed struggle' if attacked by US Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro has warned that his country will shift from politics to "armed struggle" if the United States launches an attack.
  • Venezuela Starts Preparing for 'Armed Struggle,' In Case of Attack - Maduro Sputnik 06 Sep 2025 -- Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro has warned that if his country is attacked, it will move into a phase of active military preparedness to defend its national integrity and sovereignty.
  • Trump mulling strikes against Venezuela: Report Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- US President Donald Trump is considering staging a military attack on Venezuela in a fresh effort to overthrow the Caracas government.

  • Laos Could Receive Petroleum From Russia, Supply Bauxite, Potassium - Prime Minister Sputnik 06 Sep 2025 -- Laos would like to establish supplies of petroleum products from Russia and is ready to supply minerals such as bauxite or potassium for fertilizer production, Lao Prime Minister Sonexay Siphandone told Sputnik.
  • Geopolitical Game Started by West Entering Decisive Stage - Russian Presidential Adviser Sputnik 06 Sep 2025 -- The big geopolitical game that the West has started is entering a decisive stage, Anton Kobyakov, Russian presidential adviser and executive secretary of the Organizing Committee of the Eastern Economic Forum (EEF), said.

  • Chinese embassy calls US House Republicans' report targeting China-US research collaboration 'groundless' Global Times 06 Sep 2025 -- The Chinese Embassy in the US called a US House Republicans' report "groundless," as the report claimed that the Pentagon-funded projects in collaboration with Chinese universities and institutes in defense industry have allowed "China to exploit US research partnerships for military gains," according to AP on Saturday.
  • Deputy director of 14th National Committee of CPPCC economic committee under investigation for serious discipline violations Global Times 06 Sep 2025 -- Yi Huiman, deputy director of the Committee on Economic Affairs of the 14th National Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), is under investigation for serious violations of discipline and law.

  • MND monitoring Taiwan Strait amid foreign warship transit reports CNA 06 Sep 2025 -- Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense (MND) said Saturday it is closely monitoring the Taiwan Strait after foreign media reported that Canadian and Australian warships transited the waterway.
  • Era of political interference in judiciary is over: President Lai CNA 06 Sep 2025 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Saturday called for stronger public understanding and respect for the rule of law, asserting that the era of political interference in the justice system is over.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 06 Sep 2025
  • Exclusive: Canadian and Australian warships transit Taiwan Straits, PLA tracks throughout Global Times 06 Sep 2025 -- According to sources, on Saturday, Canadian frigate the HMCS Ville de Québec and the Australian guided-missile destroyer, HMAS Brisbane, transited the Taiwan Straits. The People's Liberation Army (PLA) maintained full surveillance and monitoring throughout the transit, with the situation fully under control, the Global Times learned exclusively.

  • Delegation of DPRK Ministry of Culture Leaves for Russia KCNA 06 Sep 2025 -- A delegation of the Ministry of Culture of the DPRK led by Minister Sung Jong Gyu left here on September 5 to attend the eleventh international forum of culture to be held in Sankt-Peterburg.
  • Delegation of DPRK Ministry of Public Security Returns Home KCNA 06 Sep 2025 -- A delegation of the Ministry of Public Security of the DPRK led by Minister Pang Tu Sop returned home on September 5 after visiting Russia.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Greetings from President of United Russia Party KCNA 06 Sep 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, received a message of greeting from D. Medvedev, president of the United Russia Party, on September 5.

  • US, Israel failed to degrade Iran's nuclear, missile, air defense capabilities: Army chief Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Iran's Army Chief Major General Amir Hatami says the United States and Israeli regime aimed to degrade the Islamic Republic's nuclear, missile, and defense capabilities during the 12-day imposed war in June, but failed to achieve their main goals.
  • Iran set to submit draft resolution on attacks against peaceful nuclear facilities: Sources IRNA 06 Sep 2025 -- The Islamic Republic of Iran intends to present a draft resolution at the upcoming General Conference of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) regarding the unacceptability of attacks on nuclear facilities.
  • Araghchi slams Western 'hysteria' on Iran nuclear issue, silence on Israel's arsenal IRNA 06 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi has sharply criticized what he called the "Western hysteria" over nuclear proliferation in Iran while keeping silent on Israel's expansion of its nuclear weapons arsenal.
  • Iran 'very close' to new framework for cooperation with IAEA: FM Araghchi Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Iran says it is "very close" to devising a new framework for bilateral cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), months after Israeli and American airstrikes on the country's nuclear facilities prompted it to suspend all engagement.
  • Iran's security chief calls for stronger, more tangible ties with Iraq amid global developments Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council Ali Larijani has called for expansion of more tangible bilateral relations and wide-covering cooperation with neighboring Iraq in light of unfolding global developments.
  • Araghchi: Misunderstanding with Qatar resolved IRNA 06 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi has said that all misunderstandings between Iran and Qatar have been fully resolved following his recent visit to the Persian Gulf country, describing the talks with Qatari officials as "fruitful, serious, and constructive."
  • Araghchi holds phone discussions with Greek, Slovenian, and Sierra Leonean counterparts IRNA 06 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi has held separate phone conversations with his Greek, Slovenian, and Sierra Leonean counterparts regarding bilateral relations and regional and international developments, particularly the recent letter by Britain, Germany, and France to the UN Security Council seeking to revive terminated resolutions against Iran.
  • Electricity available to 99.8% of Iran's villages: Official Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Iran's Energy Ministry has further expanded electricity penetration across the country, as an official says that some 99.8% of villages in Iran have access to power.
  • Iran reports significant rise in exports to African countries Press TV 06 Sep 2025 -- Iran has reported a significant rise in exports to Africa amid efforts by the country to diversify its export markets and to reduce its dependence on direct oil exports.

  • Statement of the Executive Secretary Philippines DFA 06 Sep 2025 -- The Cabinet strongly objects to the recent spins coming from certain members of the House of Representatives who are thereby attempting to shift the blame for their own corruption and failures onto the Executive Branch.

  • Trump Says He Believes Belarus To Release Hundreds Of Political Prisoners In 'Near Future' RFE/RL 06 Sep 2025 -- US President Donald Trump, who surprised many by speaking to Belarus ruler Aleksandr Lukashenko by phone last month, said he believes the Eastern European nation will be releasing many of the hundreds of political prisoners it is holding.

  • Prime Minister holds a telephone call with President Macron India PIB 06 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held a telephone conversation with H
  • Deeply appreciate and fully reciprocate President Trump's sentiments: PM India PIB 06 Sep 2025 -- The Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi has stated that he deeply appreciate and fully reciprocate President Trump's sentiments and positive assessment of India-US ties

  • Police In Novi Sad Use Force To Respond To Student Protesters RFE/RL 06 Sep 2025 -- Hundreds of police used tear gas and stun grenades to disperse protesters around the Novi Sad university campus on September 5 as students gathered to demand reforms.
